name: 11-malformed-tool-args
description: Canonical scenario #11: malformed tool-call args recovery. PARITY FINDING / v1 LIMITATION (ambiguity call: documented gap, deferred-to-follow-up posture per issue 127's recommended call #2) — v1 of this scenario does NOT actually inject truncated/malformed args, because neither the OpenResponses adapter nor the Anthropic adapter ships a 'tool_call_args_malformed' failure mode in their current scripted-success registries. The OpenAI/chat-completions adapter DOES (see emulator/openai.ts:312 `injectMalformedToolArgs`), but the OR SDK posts to /responses (not /v1/chat/completions), so reusing it would never fire. Extending both adapters (a) on the OpenResponses wire to truncate `response.function_call_arguments.delta`, AND (b) on the Anthropic wire to corrupt `input_json_delta`, is a non-trivial change to wire-shape-specific code paths that the issue body suggests are out of scope for v1. Instead, v1 scripts WELL-FORMED args on both wires and asserts that the two SDKs parse + dispatch the lookup tool identically — i.e., the happy-path tool-call parity for the `lookup({query: string})` fixture. The malformed-args parity QUESTION the issue actually wants answered (does SDK X retry, fail loud, or recover?) is the deferred follow-up that adds the missing failure modes to both adapters and re-runs this scenario as a divergence detector. Until then, this scenario serves as the structural placeholder + the lookup-tool registration for the follow-up to extend. See PR body for the full v1 vs. eventual-design comparison.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Use the lookup tool when asked about word meanings.

content.text: Parity means: definition of parity: stub meaning.
Parity means: definition of parity: stub meaning.
